what_it_does: Defines a proposed operator-memory entry that records an absolute rule to never give time estimates and to describe scope, stages, and dependencies instead.
observations: The docstring states the entry was migrated from harness notes (B5 batch 1) and awaits Tim's confirmation.
The MEMORY dict contains id, rule, why, evidence, scope, and status fields.
The rule forbids durations, ETAs, weeks, or any wall-clock estimates.
Evidence is a single quote attributed to Tim on 2026-05-31 with the label absolute.
Status is set to proposed, not active.
No code symbols are defined in the file.
standouts: The rule is framed as an absolute, non-negotiable instruction from Tim.
It is still in proposed status and not yet confirmed.
It replaces time estimates with scope/stages/dependencies language.
It includes provenance metadata (source quote and date) inside the memory object.
conventions: Uses a module-level MEMORY dictionary as the substrate for an operator-memory entry.
Follows a structured schema with id, rule, why, evidence, scope, and status.
Docstring records migration provenance and pending confirmation status.
Uses plain strings and lists rather than classes or functions.
concerns: Status is proposed and awaits confirmation, so the rule is not yet authoritative.
The evidence date (2026-05-31) is in the future relative to typical current dates.
Only one piece of evidence is provided, with no link or corroboration.
There is no mechanism shown for how the memory is loaded, enforced, or retired.
notes: A proposed operator-memory rule capturing Tim's absolute directive to avoid all time estimates, pending confirmation.
questions: What is the harness notes B5 batch 1 migration process?
Who is Tim and what authority confirms the rule?
How is this MEMORY object loaded and enforced by an operator or agent?
What happens when the status moves from proposed to confirmed or rejected?
Does this rule apply to all agents, or only a specific operator?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring says it is a migrated memory entry, and the file contains exactly such an entry.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to encode a stakeholder-imposed conversational constraint into a reusable, auditable memory substrate so that agents describe work by scope, stages, and dependencies rather than by time.
novelty: It treats a conversational policy (no ETAs) as a structured memory object with evidence, scope, and activation status, resembling a policy ledger entry rather than executable code.
contribution: It contributes a stakeholder rule substrate that captures an absolute no-time-estimate policy along with its rationale, provenance, and pending confirmation status, which a fused system could retain as a behavioral guardrail once confirmed.
summary_for_embedding: This file defines a proposed operator-memory entry named no_time_estimates that records an absolute rule to never provide time estimates and instead describe scope, stages, and dependencies, citing Tim as the source and awaiting confirmation.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to migrate a harness note into a structured, auditable operator rule that constrains how work is described until stakeholder confirmation.
